summary: Strong second‑set highlights: Eyes Of The World, Picasso Moon, Dark Star tease
blurb: The second set shines with an expansive Eyes Of The World, a soaring Picasso Moon into Crazy Fingers, and a dark‑star‑style jam inside Wharf Rat. Jerry’s guitar teases Dark Star while Phil drives the groove; keyboard density is noticeable but doesn’t drown out the band.
review: June 28, 1991 at Mile High Stadium captured the Dead in a high‑energy swing through their early‑’90s catalog. The first set delivered solid crowd‑pleasers like Touch Of Grey and Iko Iko, but it’s the second half that truly stands out – an extended Eyes Of The World rolls into a luminous Picasso Moon that blossoms into Crazy Fingers, followed by a dark‑star‑flavored Wharf Rat jam before the band rockets through Throwing Stones and Not Fade Away. Jerry Garcia’s tone was described as “perfect,” with his Dark Star tease echoing through the Wharf Rat jam, while Phil Lesh laid down a frenetic bass pattern that propelled the set forward. The keyboards (Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick) were noted as dense, yet they added texture to the Picasso Moon/ Crazy Fingers sequence. Mickey Hart’s drumming was highlighted for its sheer volume wave, and Billy Kreutzmann kept the rhythmic foundation solid throughout. The encore closed with a heartfelt Box of Rain, rounding out a show that, despite some mix‑balance quirks, remains a memorable snapshot of the band’s late‑career improvisational prowess.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Mixed and negative reviews are balanced by several strongly positive comments about the second‑set jams, Dark Star tease, and overall energy; net sentiment is modestly positive.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with generally clear, crisp transfer; captures the standout moments (Dark Star tease, Eyes Of The World, Picasso Moon) without major drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Extended Eyes Of The World that builds into a luminous Picasso Moon > Crazy Fingers jam
Dark Star‑style tease inside Wharf Rat creating a memorable improvisational peak
Solid Box of Rain encore and overall high energy second set despite dense keyboard mix

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar tone praised as perfect; Dark Star tease inside Wharf Rat and spine‑tingling opening set the night on fire.
band_performance.Phil: Frenetic bass pattern drives the second set’s momentum; solid foundation on Box of Rain encore.
band_performance.Bob: Adds vocal texture, noted for mimicking a screaming fan during Estimated Prophet (though not in setlist) and supports the jam flow.
band_performance.Bruce Hornsby: Keyboard work described as dense and sometimes fighting for bandwidth, yet contributes to the dynamic Picasso Moon/ Crazy Fingers section.
band_performance.Mickey: Drum volume described as a powerful wave; facial expression became a running joke among fans.
band_performance.Billy: Keeps steady rhythmic backbone throughout, complementing Mickey’s expansive playing.
